verses.text: Moses says: but they won't believe me. They will say the LORD has not appeared to you. Guidance: Doubt about how you will be received is normal. Bring it to God instead of carrying it into the room with you.

What is that in your hand? A rod. Throw it down. It becomes a serpent; Moses runs from it. Pick it up by the tail. He does — and it is a rod again. This is so they may believe the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Guidance: God starts with what is already in your hand. The first miracle uses the staff Moses brought with him.

What is that in your hand? A rod. Throw it down. It becomes a serpent; Moses runs from it. Pick it up by the tail. He does — and it is a rod again. This is so they may believe the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Guidance: God starts with what is already in your hand. The first miracle uses the staff Moses brought with him.

What is that in your hand? A rod. Throw it down. It becomes a serpent; Moses runs from it. Pick it up by the tail. He does — and it is a rod again. This is so they may believe the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Guidance: God starts with what is already in your hand. The first miracle uses the staff Moses brought with him.

What is that in your hand? A rod. Throw it down. It becomes a serpent; Moses runs from it. Pick it up by the tail. He does — and it is a rod again. This is so they may believe the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Guidance: God starts with what is already in your hand. The first miracle uses the staff Moses brought with him.

Put your hand into your bosom. He does, and his hand comes out leprous, white as snow. Put it back in. He does, and it is restored. Guidance: God will sometimes show you how easily He can wound and heal. The same hand that brings disease can also restore. Power belongs to Him alone.

Put your hand into your bosom. He does, and his hand comes out leprous, white as snow. Put it back in. He does, and it is restored. Guidance: God will sometimes show you how easily He can wound and heal. The same hand that brings disease can also restore. Power belongs to Him alone.

If they will not believe even these two signs, take water from the Nile and pour it on dry ground; it will become blood. Guidance: God patiently builds evidence for those slow to believe. He overdoes the proof on purpose — He knows the heart's resistance.

If they will not believe even these two signs, take water from the Nile and pour it on dry ground; it will become blood. Guidance: God patiently builds evidence for those slow to believe. He overdoes the proof on purpose — He knows the heart's resistance.

O my Lord, says Moses — I am not eloquent. I never have been; I am slow of speech and slow of tongue. Guidance: Naming your weakness honestly is not faithlessness. But once you have named it, listen to what God says next.

The LORD: who made the human mouth? Who makes the mute, the deaf, the seeing, the blind? Is it not I, the LORD? Now go; I will be with your mouth and teach you what to say. Guidance: God does not deny your limitation — He claims authorship over it. The mouth He made, He can also use.

The LORD: who made the human mouth? Who makes the mute, the deaf, the seeing, the blind? Is it not I, the LORD? Now go; I will be with your mouth and teach you what to say. Guidance: God does not deny your limitation — He claims authorship over it. The mouth He made, He can also use.

O my Lord, send by another hand. The LORD's anger kindles: is not Aaron your brother? I know he can speak well; he is coming to meet you already, and his heart will be glad to see you. Guidance: When you keep declining, God provides a partner instead of withdrawing the call. Anger and provision in the same breath — both signs of love.

O my Lord, send by another hand. The LORD's anger kindles: is not Aaron your brother? I know he can speak well; he is coming to meet you already, and his heart will be glad to see you. Guidance: When you keep declining, God provides a partner instead of withdrawing the call. Anger and provision in the same breath — both signs of love.

You shall speak to him and put words in his mouth. I will be with your mouth and his, and will teach you both what to do. He shall be your spokesman to the people; he shall be your mouth, and you shall be to him as God. Guidance: Ministry is often partnered. The gift you lack is in someone already on the way to you.

You shall speak to him and put words in his mouth. I will be with your mouth and his, and will teach you both what to do. He shall be your spokesman to the people; he shall be your mouth, and you shall be to him as God. Guidance: Ministry is often partnered. The gift you lack is in someone already on the way to you.

And take this rod in your hand — you will do signs with it. Guidance: Whatever ordinary thing God has given you, He may use to do extraordinary things. The shepherd's stick becomes the rod that parts the sea.

Moses returns to Jethro, his father-in-law: let me go and see whether my brethren in Egypt are still alive. Jethro: go in peace. Guidance: Obedience often begins by asking permission of those you owe respect. Even Moses needs his father-in-law's blessing before he leaves.

The LORD tells Moses in Midian: return to Egypt — all the men who sought your life are dead. Moses takes his wife and sons, sets them on a donkey, and returns to Egypt, with the rod of God in his hand. Guidance: God closes old threats before opening new doors. The fear of forty years ago no longer stands between you and the call.

The LORD tells Moses in Midian: return to Egypt — all the men who sought your life are dead. Moses takes his wife and sons, sets them on a donkey, and returns to Egypt, with the rod of God in his hand. Guidance: God closes old threats before opening new doors. The fear of forty years ago no longer stands between you and the call.

When you return, see that you do all the wonders before Pharaoh — but I will harden his heart so he will not let the people go. Tell him: Israel is My son, My firstborn. Let My son go, that he may serve Me; if you refuse, I will slay your firstborn. Guidance: God names the conflict clearly before it begins. The deliverance He plans is also a costly one — for the oppressor and his household.

When you return, see that you do all the wonders before Pharaoh — but I will harden his heart so he will not let the people go. Tell him: Israel is My son, My firstborn. Let My son go, that he may serve Me; if you refuse, I will slay your firstborn. Guidance: God names the conflict clearly before it begins. The deliverance He plans is also a costly one — for the oppressor and his household.

When you return, see that you do all the wonders before Pharaoh — but I will harden his heart so he will not let the people go. Tell him: Israel is My son, My firstborn. Let My son go, that he may serve Me; if you refuse, I will slay your firstborn. Guidance: God names the conflict clearly before it begins. The deliverance He plans is also a costly one — for the oppressor and his household.

On the way, at a lodging place, the LORD meets Moses and seeks to kill him. Zipporah takes a sharp stone, circumcises her son, touches Moses' feet with the foreskin, and says: surely a bridegroom of blood art thou to me. So He lets him go. Guidance: Moses had neglected his son's circumcision; his wife saves his life by stepping in. Even the deliverer is held to the covenant. Faithfulness at home matters as much as faithfulness on the mission.

On the way, at a lodging place, the LORD meets Moses and seeks to kill him. Zipporah takes a sharp stone, circumcises her son, touches Moses' feet with the foreskin, and says: surely a bridegroom of blood art thou to me. So He lets him go. Zipporah takes a sharp stone, circumcises her son, touches Moses' feet with the foreskin, and says: surely a bridegroom of blood art thou to me. So He lets him go. Guidance: Moses had neglected his son's circumcision; his wife saves his life by stepping in. Even the deliverer is held to the covenant. Faithfulness at home matters as much as faithfulness on the mission.

The LORD tells Aaron: go to meet Moses. He goes, finds him at the mountain of God, and kisses him. Moses tells Aaron all the LORD's words and all the signs. Guidance: God arranges reunions you didn't ask for. The brother who has been gone forty years walks back into your life at exactly the right hour.

The LORD tells Aaron: go to meet Moses. He goes, finds him at the mountain of God, and kisses him. Moses tells Aaron all the LORD's words and all the signs. Guidance: God arranges reunions you didn't ask for. The brother who has been gone forty years walks back into your life at exactly the right hour.

Moses and Aaron gather the elders. Aaron speaks all the words; Moses performs the signs. The people believe — and when they hear that the LORD has visited them and seen their affliction, they bow their heads and worship. Guidance: News of God's nearness draws worship before it brings deliverance. Belief comes first; the actual exodus is still chapters away.
